name: Graph Traversal (BFS / DFS)
description: Graph traversal visits every reachable vertex from a start node using a worklist: breadth-first search uses a FIFO queue to explore level by level, depth-first search uses a LIFO stack to dive deep first; vertices are marked visited to avoid revisiting, and traversal ends when the container is empty.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Graph + start vertex"]
    N2["Mark start visited"]
    N3{"BFS or DFS?"}
    N4["BFS: enqueue (queue)"]
    N5["DFS: push (stack)"]
    N6["Remove next vertex"]
    N7["Visit unvisited neighbors"]
    N8{"Container empty?"}
    N9["Add neighbor to container"]
    N10["All reachable visited"]
    N11["Traversal order"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|bfs| N4
    N3 -->|dfs| N5
    N4 --> N6
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|continue| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Introduction to Algorithms (4th ed.)
sources.authors: Cormen, T. H.; Leiserson, C. E.; Rivest, R. L.; Stein, C.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
